free_text: Nuclear level-density parameters of nuclei in the Z
= 70 and A = 180 mid-shell regions
The experiment was performed using the
heavy-ion beams from the BARC-TIFR 14-MV Pelletron
accelerator facility at Mumbai
The alpha particles emitted in
the reactions were detected by two silicon surface
barrier dE-E (28 mu-m and 2 mm thick) detector
telescopes mounted in a reaction plane at theta lab
= 125deg and 153deg with respect to beam direction.
Gamma-ray multiplicity setup consisting of 14
bismuth germinate detectors was used for gamma ray
multiplicity measurement
Surface-barrier detector with solid angle of
0.20 mSr was mounted at theta lab = 16deg to measure
Rutherford scattering events for normalization and
cross-section calculation.
No information given
Compiled by S.H.
Multiplicity (fold) of gamma rays coincident
with alpha particle with energy E. Energy of
gamma rays was not measured.
Center-of-mass alpha particle energy spectra measured
at given angle for given gamma ray multiplicity MISC.

Self-supporting 164Dy metallic foils with thickness
of  1.1 mg/cm2.
